observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of the affected Property, Plant and Equipment account as at the end of the year and transfer the said accounts to the books of the barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue demand letters to the Accountable Officers concerned for the immediate submission of Liquidation Reports and other supporting documents and/or the return of unexpended/excess cash advance, if any. In case of failure of the AOs concerned to comply, require the Municipal Accountant or Payroll Officer to withhold their salaries until compliance is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry and ensure the complete and timely recording of the transactions, particularly the grant and liquidation of cash advances so that the assets acquired or expenses incurred out of the cash advances are duly recorded in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the two check payments of which DVs and supporting documents were already submitted; and b) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RCI on a daily basis, and to ensure that the DVs and supporting documents for all the checks released during the day are included in the transmittal to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Reconcile balances of Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts and effect the adjustment in the financial statements; b) Consider the preparation and maintenance of individual subsidiary ledgers for all suppliers and employees to ensure accurate reliable withholding and remittance of taxes, contributions, and loan amortization.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording of intra-agency transfer of DRRM funds for CY 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Comply with the disclosure requirements of the pertinent IPSAS; and b) Provide other information relevant to other balances in the FS that are required to be disclosed by the applicable guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Local Development Council and other officials concerned, ensure careful planning and determination of priority development projects under 20% Development Fund such that all PPAs that are considered for funding thereunder are well-planned-and-implementation-ready, and the funds are optimally utilized so that the benefits to the people are timely delivered.

Sagguniang Bayan and LDRRM Council: a) Coordinate with the DENR and comply the requirements for the immediate continuation of the causeway project, otherwise reconsider the funds for other priority project; and b) Henceforth carefully plan, evaluate and monitor the programs, projects, and activities embodied in the LDRRM investment Plan ensuring their proper and timely implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officers concerned to ensure that project endorsed and implemented under the SBDP of NTF ELCAC are free from any issue on land/site to avoid adverse consequences the Municipal Mayor should also direct:

a. The Municipal Engineer to require the contractors of subsequent infrastructure projects, including those under the same program herein, to comply with the appropriate size of signboards and the updating of the complete status and information therein;

b. The Municipal engineer to ensure complete disclosure in the Inspection Report of the works accomplished and necessary rectification, if any, to provide basis to the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant on the propriety of approval and processing payments.

c. The Municipal Accountant to present the lacking supporting documents; and

d. The local MENRO to coordinate with the EMB/DENR officials for the needed permit and clearance for the causeway project in order to complete the project.

We recommended that Local Executive Chief stop the payment of overtime “premiums” to Job Order workers and payments be made only on the basis of daily wage rate provided in the approved Job Order contract.

We recommended that management comply with the rules and regulations on the employee’s monthly net take home pay, pursuant to the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act, which the same shall in no case be lower than the required minimum amount specified therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting entries for the reclassification of the structure to more appropriate account and recognition of impairment losses, as well as to provide relevant disclosures in the Notes to FS;

b. the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately initiate the inventory and disposal of the structure pursuant to the procedural guidelines of COA Circular No. 89-296, and prepare all required documents and reports of submission to the Audit Team and/or Technical Property Inspector; and

c. the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Engineer, and other employees concerned to coordinate in the preparation of the necessary plans and reports to be presented to the appropriate Committee and the Sanggunian for the funding of the infrasture project for demolition, subject to compliance with other recommendations by the Technical and Audit Teams as a result of evaluation and inspection of the property for demolition.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. The Bids and Award Committee to set standard of costing vehicle rentals, ensuring judicious and economic use of funds for said services;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the PESO Manager to immediately submit photocopy of valid IDs of the beneficiaries and other documents which show result of the profiling and the release of amounts to proper claimants.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately initiate action to enforce settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances of officials/employees concerned in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officers/employees to act immediately on the preparation of the required Work and Financial Plan and other documents for the immediate utilization/return/reconciliation of the above funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Sanggunian, Municipal Treasurer and other officials/employees concerned coordinate with each other and formulate strategies to improve collections of RPT and SET. Likewise, we recommend that management avail all of the legal remedies provided under RA 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU, in coordination with the Provincial Government.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting
entries for the reclassification of the structure to more appropriate
account and recognition of impairment losses, as well as to provide
relevant disclosures in the Notes to FS;
b. the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately initiate the
inventory and disposal of the structure pursuant to the procedural
guidelines of COA Circular No. 89-296, and prepare all required
documents and reports for submission to the Audit Team and/or
Technical Property Inspector; and
c. the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal
Engineer, and other employees concerned to coordinate in the
preparation of the necessary plans and reports to be presented to the
appropriate Committee and the Sanggunian for the funding of the
infrastructure project for demolition, subject to compliance with other
recommendation/s by the Technical and Audit Teams as a result of
evaluation and inspection of the property for demolition.

2.6. We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to reconcile their records on collections in order to reflect the
accurate balance of the Cash Local Treasury account, and henceforth
require them to conduct at least a quarterly reconciliation of their balances.

3.10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a. Provide a mechanism for the immediate release of checks already
signed and approved, that will likewise ensure that they are released
only to payees thereof or their authorized representatives;
b. Immediately secure an official receipt upon release of the check and/or
the signature of the payee on the proper portion of the DV;
c. Prepare the RCI on a daily basis and effect the immediate submission
to the Municipal Accountant of the corresponding paid Disbursement
Vouchers and their supporting documents; and
d. Prepare and submit report of unreleased checks, if any, at every
submission thereof.

We recommended that:
a. The Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to formulate
a revised Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) ensuring first the completeness of
records from the Accounting and Property Units which shall serve as
inventory working papers;
b. The Local Chief Executive authorize, as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2020-006, the committee members to be temporarily relieved of all their
regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical
inventory taking until the same is completed;
c. The Inventory Committee to carefully and completely execute the
formulated Physical Inventory Plan to come up with and Inventory Report
that contains all the necessary data/information that will facilitate
reconciliation of records, and thus the cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Municipal Agriculture Officer submit to the required Inventory
Report, on a regular basis, at least once a year, which shall include all
breeding stocks and other biological assets owned and controlled by the
Municipality, to provide the Municipal Accountant the necessary data
and information on any changes that should be accounted for in the books
of accounts;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry, and
ensure the complete accounting of the changes in Biological Assets based
on the reports submitted by the MAO.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the
completeness and full disclosure of financial transactions in the financial
statements and in the Notes to Financial Statements are religiously complied,
to provide the users of financial reports, all the necessary information on the
receipt and utilization of funds.

We recommended that management: a. Initiate the process and procedures on the write-off of the pertinent receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, and the long-overdue receivables be provided with Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible receivables pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivables accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts. b. Provide allowance for impairment corresponding to the uncollectible receivables.
observations.managementAction: Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible receivables was provided. Requests and supporting documents to write-off the long-overdue receivables are yet to be completed and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipal Accountant for the substantial compliance with the
timelines in the preparation and submission of accounts and financial reports. We
recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue her utmost commitment to
the professional ethics and standards for Government Accountants.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee come up with alternative
procedures to complete the inventory taking with consideration of the most
ideal period of conducting the planned activities. Furthermore, we
recommend that the Local Chief Executive authorize, as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2020-006, the committee members to be temporarily relieved of
all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical
inventory taking until the same is complied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the contract awardees for
the procurement of goods understand and comply with the terms and
conditions provided in the contract. Moreover, consider in the post-
qualification of bids and evaluation of received quotations, for purposes
of awarding the contracts, the performance and compliance to contract
terms of bidder-supplier in past transactions. Further, make sure that the
PO should be properly accomplished to show relevant and complete
information; and
b. The Municipal Accountant ensure that penalties as a result of delays in the
delivery of goods by suppliers are computed and deducted from any
money due or which may become due to them.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) stop the
practice of piecemeal procurement of supplies and materials and adopt
competitive bidding in compliance with RA 9184. We further recommend
that the BAC Chairman, its members, and Technical Working Group
(TWG) formulate and implement strategic plans and approaches to ensure
efficiency and economy in the procurement of goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to revisit the stipulations in the
Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and source agencies to
properly identify the required/applicable mode of disposition of the
unutilized balance of fund transfers, and require them to immediately
comply as stipulated.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly require all the
Revenue Collectors to turnover daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer,
all collections in accordance with Sections 26 and 33 of COA Circular No. 92-
382 and Sections 60 and 61 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.
Moreover, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), through the
Treasurer’s Office, provide measures and mechanisms that will ensure strict
enforcement of rules and regulations in the remittance of collections,
especially by the deputized collectors from the Barangay, such as non-
issuance of accountable form (AF) unless the previously issued AF has been
remitted to and properly accounted for by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to release to the Barangay
Treasurers, on a quarterly basis, the barangay share from RPT, Community
Tax and PCA collections for the immediate use of the additional fund for
their operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that actions needed
to be taken for the immediate release by the Source Agency, and
disbursement of future grants and benefits to health workers, or any other
group of beneficiaries, be accomplished or done with urgency so that
recipients of such grants will be able to immediately benefit from the purpose
for which the funds are intended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with requirement of
Section 37(C), Rule VI of RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women and
Section 4.1.A of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, on the creation
of the GAD Focal Point System.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning
& Development Officer, Municipal Engineer, and other implementers to
coordinate with each other to fast track and monitor the immediate
completion of these development projects, ensuring their usability, sound
structure, and availability to the general public or its intended beneficiaries,
so that these projects can contribute to the socio-economic recovery of the
municipality and its people in these difficult times.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Council optimize the use of its DRRM
funds for capital outlay to ensure that the necessary rescue and response
equipment are procured and completed to enable the municipality to be
optimally prepared for any disaster or calamity.

We recommended that the LCE: a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct immediate taking of PPE. b. Initiate the formulation of plans/strategies for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee formulated its PIP and started the inventory taking of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the issuance/distribution of goods to the beneficiaries. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile Accounting records with the results of the physical count to fairly present the effects of transactions on the agency’s assets, liabilities and equity in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was prepared to correct the inventory accounts. RIS and RSMI distributed to beneficiaries. Inventory accounts were maintained for goods distributed to beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require: a. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to conduct immediate verification of the existence and condition of the stale checks, and whether or not replacement shall still be required. b. The Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to restore the liabilities of the LGU to the payees/creditors for checks which shall still require replacement, by debiting Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable. For checks which were already cancelled and replaced, debit Cash in Bank - LCCA and credit Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries. The management will conduct further verification. No demand received from any payee for replacement of stale checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a. The Municipal Treasurer to issue Billing Statements for defaulting Market Stall Holders and furnish copies to the Municipal Accountant for recording and monitor collections thereof; b. The Municipal Accountant to record the receivables and income based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Billing statements to defaulting stall holders were issued and sent to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation between the two accounts and make the necessary adjusting entry to ensure that movements made on the receivable account in the transferring fund have corresponding movements on the payable account in the receiving fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared a reconciliation of reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and made necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents; b. Instruct the concerned officials in the procurement process to ensure that the LGU only transact business with suppliers/service providers in the legitimate capacity.
observations.managementAction: The lacking supporting documents were submitted to the audit team. The suppliers were required of the permits/licenses to support technical and financial capacity. The concerned suppliers were required to submit documents; the audit team received the supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a. The Local Finance Committee to prepare the reports showing the details of utilization of the Bayanihan grant and post the same; b. The BAC Secretariat to post the details of the awarded contracts in the GPPB Online Portal and accumulated reports on utilization of Bayanihan grant in at least three (3) conspicuous public places and submit the said reports or the information on the compliance to posting and reporting requirements to other offices where submission is required.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee prepared detailed statement of reports utilization of Bayanihan grant amounting to full disclosure policy board. The report was submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of granting cash advances for the payment of salaries and wages, commutable benefits, travel allowances, honoraria and other benefits except for operating and petty operating expenses which allowed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant no longer allowed cash advances except for salaries, wages, other personnel commutable benefits, travel allowances, honoraria and other benefits. Henceforth, the Municipality shall strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, RA 9184 and its IRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: a. Require the Disposal Committee to prepare the IIRUP and initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386; b. After the disposal, require the Disposal Committee to submit the necessary documents, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance with the approved accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Some unserviceable PPE were already disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts. Disposal Committee submitted the necessary documents, the basis to drop the unserviceable properties from the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant require contractors concerned to post the required warranty securities. Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.
observations.managementAction: Warranty securities were required from contractors. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities. Henceforth, the Municipality shall strictly enforce compliance with pertinent provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the second phase of the building be implemented immediately to prevent prolonged exposure of the Ply board flooring to the adverse effects of the natural elements and for beneficial use of the building by its targeted beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The phase 2 of the building was implemented but the renovated part remained not used and the plyboard flooring not yet replaced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and BPLO designate to require a duly executed lease contract between the occupants of the Merkado Stalls and Market Tienda rooms before a business permit is renewed in order that the leasehold agreement be legally binding between the parties.
observations.managementAction: Market stalls and Market Tienda rooms occupied by vendors and business owners are already covered by lease contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set on their submission.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for CY 2021 were submitted within the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council consider the inclusion in their plans the programs, projects and activities that will provide impact on the welfare of the constituents, as well as consider implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The continuing appropriations were mostly implemented. Funds in the prior years were not fully utilized to implement various other priority PPAs. Some prior years’ PPAs were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE through the Municipal Officials: a. To prepare the LDRRMF IP which includes PPAs for additional PPAs or changes in PPAs during the year. b. To prepare a revised/supplemental LDRRMF IP in case additional PPAs are authorized and such were not included in the approved AIP; c. To ensure the efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities is achieved.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF IP already included additional PPAs and changes in PPAs during the year. MDRRMO minimized the utilization of LDRRM funds for administrative purposes. MDRRMO is required to submit the budgetary proposal for the regular operation of the MDRRM Office so that the 5% calamity fund should be exclusively appropriated and utilized for expenses directly related to disaster risk-reduction management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Officer: to prepare the Special Trust Fund, some PPAs for additional PPAs which includes PPAs and to ensure efficient utilization of the LDRRM fund for disaster preparedness and response.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO minimized the utilization of LDRRM funds for administrative purposes. MDRRMO was required to submit budgetary proposals for the regular operation of the MDRRM Office so that the 5% calamity fund should be exclusively appropriated and utilized for expenses directly related to disaster risk-reduction management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. The concerned persons accountable to liquidate their outstanding cash advances or delete their names from the payroll until their cash advances are fully settled; b. The Municipal Accountant to fully apply proceeds of any claims/amounts due the persons accountable concerned, to their respective cash advances if their net take home pay is insufficient.
observations.managementAction: Some long overdue outstanding cash advances were not liquidated during the year. The Municipal Accountant implemented withholding of outstanding cash advances in installments until the time that the cash advances are liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the LGU Officials to fast track the implementation of the Capital Outlay PPAs of the DRRM fund. Henceforth implement all LDRMM –PPAs as programmed in the AIP and LDRRMF Investment Plan in order to enhance the LGU’s pre-disaster preparedness and post-disaster response capability in times of calamities.
observations.managementAction: The capital outlay DRRM fund was utilized to implemented current and prior years’ appropriations, and DRRM operations. Some capital outlay PPAs during CY 2021 were not implemented. The funds were not optimally utilized to procure the needed equipage for DRRM operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE through the Municipal Assessor: a. Take appropriate steps in order to attain 100% revision of all Tax Declarations of all RPUs in the municipality and submit the updated records to the Provincial Assessor as pre-requisite to the next General Revision; b. Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor so that the 4th General Revision of Assessments and Property Classification be made as early as possible.
observations.managementAction: Data for the 4th revision has been gathered and finalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE and the SB should appropriate the needed funds for its ESWMP in order to achieve a systematic and timely phase by phase 10-year implementation of the 10-year plan for the benefit of the general public.
observations.managementAction: Some PPAs contained in its 10-year ESWMP were implemented during the year. The MENRO was required to ensure implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 12 Paragraph (c) of Republic Act No. 10121 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC Nos. 2014-1 and 2012-02. The MDRRMO should be required to submit budgetary proposal for the regular operation of the MDRRM Office so that the 5% calamity fund should be exclusively appropriated and utilized for expenses directly related to disaster risk-reduction management.
observations.reason: The Audit Team observed that various travel expenses by the MDRRMO, office supplies and repair of the MDRRM Office were still charged to the DRRM Funds.
The PPAs under the 20% DF could not be immediately implemented due to the restrictions on the release of funds as provided in Section 2 of the Appropriation Ordinance for annual budget of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE and the SB should appropriate the needed funds for its ESWMP in order to achieve a systematic and timely phase by phase 10-year implementation of the plan for the benefit of the general public.
observations.reason: Proposed establishment of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) could not be realized due to PS limitations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the issuance/distribution of goods to the beneficiaries. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile Accounting records with the results of the physical count to fairly present the effects of transactions on the agency’s assets, liabilities and equity in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to conduct immediate verification of the existence and condition of the stale checks, and whether or not replacement shall still be required. Pursuant to the Local Treasury Operations Manual, a new check may be issued after the stale check has been submitted by the payee to the Local Treasurer, marked “CANCELLED” and the authenticity of the claim established.
The Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to restore the liabilities of the LGU to the payees/creditors for checks which shall still require replacement, by debiting Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable.  For checks which were already cancelled and replaced, debit Cash in Bank - LCCA and credit Government Equity.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The Municipal Treasurer to issue Billing Statements for defaulting Market Stall Holders and furnish copies to the Municipal Accountant for recording and monitor collections thereof.
The Municipal Accountant to record the receivables and income based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation between the two accounts and make the necessary adjusting entry to ensure that movements made on the receivable account in the transferring fund have corresponding movements on the payable account in the receiving fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of granting cash advances for the purpose other than for the payment of salaries and wages, commutable allowances, honoraria and other benefits and petty operating expenses which cannot be paid by check. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, RA 9184 and its IRR and other GPPB related issuances on the procurement of goods and services.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities.
Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

We recommended that the second phase of the building be implemented immediately to prevent prolonged exposure of the plyboard flooring to the adverse effects of natural elements and for beneficial use of the building of its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and BPLO designate to require a duly executed  lease contract  between the occupants of the Merkado Stalls and Market Tienda rooms before a business permit is renewed in order that the leasehold agreement be legally binding between the parties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set on their submission.

We recommended that the Local Development Council consider the inclusion in their plans the programs, projects and activities that will provide impact on the welfare of the constituents, as well as health and environmental issues to control the spread of the virus, consistent with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, and implement the PPAs under the 20% DF as scheduled.

We recommended that the Accountant prepare journal entries to record the valid reconciling items in the books in order to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account as well as the other accounts affected.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries.
Adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account to Road Network was already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant prepare JEV to record the reciprocal account in the Trust Fund books to take up the transfer of deposit from the General Fund in order to correct the balance of Due to NGAs account.
observations.managementAction: Reciprocal accounts were already recorded in the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to record the payment/amortization of the loans in order to reflect the accurate balance of Loans Payable- Domestic – Current, and interest expense in the Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: Payment of loans through automatic debit was already recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entry to transfer the amount of P1,087,942.45 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund to correct the balance of Trust Liability-LDRRM account.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 yearend unexpended amounts was already transferred to the Trust Fund.  Likewise, the CY 2020 year-end unexpended balance was transferred to TF before the FS was finalized and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

initiate the process and procedures on the write-off of the pertinent receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, and,
provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivables accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
observations.reason: The allowance for impairment was already provided to the uncollectible receivable accounts, however, management has not yet initiated for its write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entry to close the Buildings and Hospital & Health Centers accounts of P745,988.20 to Government Equity to correct the balance of Buildings and
 Other Structures account and transfer the said accounts to the books of the barangays.
observations.managementAction: The cost of labor for the construction of the barangay health center was already transferred to the books of the barangays.

observations.managementAction: The amounts downloaded by the DOH for the Maternal Care Package and Professional Fees were already recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

Create an Inventory Committee of three members for each office consisting of the LCE representative as chairman, the GSO/Municipal Treasurer and designated Supply Officer of each office as members.
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count for all PPEs and Investment Property, and prepare RPCPPE and RPCLRN to be submitted to the LCE, Accounting Unit, and Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

Require inventory committee to present the RPCPPE and RPCRN as to group or type of PPE and assign values thereof at cost, except for donated property which should be stated at fair value at the time of acquisition, to facilitate reconciliation with the records of the accounting unit.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was already created for the purpose.
observations.reason: (reiterated in Part II of this report, finding no. 1) | The physical count of PPE still did not include Land, Buildings and Structures and other Infrastructure Assets.  Likewise, the 2,582 items of PPE were noted missing per physical count, thus the balances remain unreconciled with the Accounting reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through its Chairman to strictly follow COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of contracts and supporting documents to facilitate the timely review of contracts so that the LGU can be immediately notified of any defects and deficiencies noted in the contract and suggest appropriate measures.
observations.managementAction: Advance copies of contracts of projects for CY 2020 were submitted to the Audit Team in a timely manner.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a printed copy of biometric records of attendance of all appointive employees be attached to the DTR in support to their claims of salaries to ensure the correctness of the manual entries thereof.

Bagacay.

observations.managementAction: Funds received from National Government Agencies were already implemented and all excess and unutilized funds were already returned to the Source Agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  Treasury Personnel to conduct a tax campaign.  To ensure collection of delinquent real property taxes, we recommend to implement the following:

Publication of Notices of Delinquency.

Levy on real property.

Sale of Property.

Collection thru courts.

Each delinquent taxpayer shall be issued with a demand letter to take notice of their payables. The application of remedies shall be carefully done seeing to it that due process of the rights of taxpayers is respected.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Accountant to verify the variance of the balances between the RPT and SET Receivables and make the appropriate adjustment if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Assessor has already conducted an intensive tax campaign which resulted in the increase of tax revenue in CY 2020 compared to CY 2019.

Implement the Rice Retailing program and Potable Water Purification System in accordance with the 

Memorandum of Agreement for the beneficial use  of the identified proponents;

Otherwise, return the funds to the source agency so that it could be reprogrammed for other PPAs of the national government.

observations.recommendation:

The former Disbursing Officer, Ma. Cristina C. Monica should be made to liquidate immediately all her outstanding cash advances.
Likewise, instruct the Accounting Office to withhold her salary  as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 until her accountability is settled.
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 which included the prohibition to grant additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance is first settled.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 particularly on the limitation on the grant of cash advance to officials and employees and observe check disbursement process to ensure proper utilization and disbursement of funds, and protect the same from risk of loss and/or misuse.

Publication of Notices of Delinquency
Levy on real property
Sale of Property
Collection thru courts

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Accountant to verify the variance of the balances between the RPT and SET Receivables and make the appropriate adjustment if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revert back to the General Fund the total amount of ₱938,735.35 representing the unexpended balances of Trust Liability-DRRM fund for the past (5) years and beyond (CYs 2011-13) and be made available for other social services as may be determined by the local sanggunian in accordance to Section 21 of RA 10121. Henceforth, revert yearly to the General Fund all maturing and unutilized DRRM funds in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that management should strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4, of PD 1445. It should observe the proper budgetary requirement for every expenditure that all disbursements should be supported with corresponding appropriation. Likewise, it should observe the provisions  contained in Section 444 of RA 7160.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 139.  Prudent use of the scarce financial resources should be observed in sending municipal officials and employees to conventions, seminars and similar activities.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended that the LCE require the collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable forms CRAAF) respectively, pursuant to Sec. 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382 in order to facilitate effective monitoring and control of the Accountable Forms of the LGU.

We recommended that management should:
Workout to have an approved 10-year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of Sections 16 and 17 of RA 9003.
Strictly enforce Section 48 of the law particularly the prohibited act of open burning of solid waste.
Likewise, strictly enforce the other relevant provisions of the law in order to attain a systematic and comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of public health and the environment

We recommended that management:
Compel the contractor to complete the construction of the two water systems for the beneficial use of the residents of the recipient communities.
Impose liquidated damages against the contractor for failure to complete the project within the contract period.
Initiate the liquidation of the Trust Liability to the DILG.

The Local Sangguniang Bayan consider the proposal and approve the same in accordance with  existing procedures.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to remit taxes withheld promptly and regularly in pursuance to the provisions of RA 8424 and the applicable BIR Rules and Regulations to address the thrust of the national government of prompt collection of taxes for its national programs.

We recommend that the LCE require the Accountant to investigate the discrepancy of balances between the subsidiary ledgers and the General Ledger for Cash in Bank –LCCA account and make the appropriate adjusting journal entries if necessary to correct the balance. Likewise, we recommend that recon-ciling items in the BRS be immediately taken up in the books and henceforth all reconciling items should be recorded first, before the books are closed and fi-nancial statements are prepared to reflect the true balance thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LCE require the Accountant to investigate the
discrepancy of balances between the subsidiary ledgers and the General Ledger
for Cash in Bank – LCCA account and make the appropriate adjusting journal
entries if necessary to correct the balance. Likewise, we recommend that
reconciling items in the BRS be immediately taken up in the books and
henceforth all reconciling items should be recorded first, before the books are
closed and financial statements are prepared to reflect the true balance thereof.

We recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to reconcile their records on collections in order to reflect the
accurate balance of the Cash Local Treasury account, and henceforth require
them to conduct at least a quarterly reconciliation of their balances.

We recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to initiate
the derecognition of the property in the books of accounts. A request to write-off
the corresponding account in the books should be submitted to the office of the
Auditor, together the relevant documents such as police report / report of
damage to property of the MDRRMO and report of Committee on Appraisal
and Disposal for any scrap material recovered and disposed.

We recommend that the LCE require the appropriate municipal officials
to initiate the processing of the TCT under the Torrens Title System for all real
properties (land) acquired that remain unregistered, in the name of the LGU in
order to obtain clean title and safeguard the assets from possible third party
claims of ownership. The needed documents should be compiled for each
property and be submitted as soon as possible to the appropriate government
offices for the purpose. We also recommend that the accountant be required to
investigate the discrepancy between the GL and SL balances, make appropriate
adjust- ing entries, as well as take up in the books the unrecorded properties in
order to come up with the correct balance of the Land account.

We recommend that management implement immediately the proposed
projects under the 20% DF for the current and prior years and henceforth
implement within the timeline or as calendared in the agency Annual Investment
Program, all development projects consistent with the general policy of the
government and mandated responsibility of LGU officials, in order to attain
desirable socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes. Likewise, we recommend that management prepare a realistic
appropriation for the different PPAs under the 20% DF, that only those PPAs
that are implementable should be provided with appropriation in order to cover
other priority PPAs which have not been funded.

We recommend that management: (a) fast track the implementation of the PPAs
consistent with the MOAs for the immediate use of the intended beneficiaries;
(b) return to the Source Agency the balance of unutilized fund for projects already
completed in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994;
(c) make appropriate adjustment for completed projects in order to recognize the
appropriate asset and equity accounts; and (d) reclassify certain amounts
improperly recorded under Due to NGAs account to their appropriate account in
the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant the compliance of COA Circular 2012-001. Provisions in the MOA
should also be enforced to submit the Fund Utilization Report together with the
other required documents as soon as projects/programs are implemented.

To avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances, we recommend that
management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) immediate settlement/liquidation of the outstanding cash advances for all
concerned accountable officers pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and
COA Circular No. 97-002;
b) strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances;
c) avoid granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance
given is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to
Section 89 of PD No. 1445;
d) initiate the filing of appropriate Administrative Proceedings for Gross
Neglect of Duty to those who failed to render account of their
accountabilities after demand or Simple Neglect of Duty to those who
failed to liquidate their cash advances in full within the prescribed
periods pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12, s. 2012;
e) exert more effort to locate the whereabouts of separated accountable
officers and issue follow-up demand letters;
f) analyze the composition of the Advances to Officers and Employees
account and reclassify in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts
for LGU for fair presentation in the financial statement; and
g) make appropriate adjustment to close the amount with negative balances
and those comprising of small amounts or fractions of centavo.

We recommend that:
c) The LSB prepare the Annual SEF Budget following the procedure set
forth in the Joint Circular and furnish the Local Accountant, Local
Treasurer and Local Budget Officer a copy of the budget;
d) Adhere to the Circular on the allowable expenses chargeable to SEF.
Furthermore, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive stop the
practice of allowing reimbursement. Instead, payments should be made by
check. Cash Advance should be granted to a Special Disbursing Officer who is
under the administrative supervision of the LGU, and not to employees of
DepEd.

We recommend that the Human Resource Management Officer and
Municipal Accountant, require all Job Order personnel to prepare and submit
duly signed and verified DTR and Job Order Contracts as basis for the
preparation of payrolls. Likewise, the accountant should require the attachment
of the certifications required.

We recommend that management should workout to have an approved
10-year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of sections 16
and 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommend that management strictly enforce
the other relevant provisions of the said law in order to attain a systematic and
comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of public health and
the environment.

We recommend that the LCE require the LGU Officials concerned to fast
track procurement of the DRRM Equipment as programmed, in order to
enhance/strengthen the LGU’s disaster preparedness and capability to respond
in times of calamities. We further recommend that unobligated Capital Outlay
appropriations recorded in the Trust Fund be returned back to the General
Fund.

We recommend that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to prepare
Agency Guidelines as bases for the preparation of the yearly GAD Plan and
Budget and Accomplishment Report, pursuant to DBM, NEDA and NCRFW
Joint Circular No. 2004-01 and the General Appropriations Act, so that GAD
related PPAs could be effectively implemented, monitored, evaluated, and
properly reported for the good and benefit of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommend that management appropriate the amount equivalent to
1% of the total IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs,
projects and activities for Local Council for the Protection of Children. We
further recommend that this program should be included in the LGUs Local
Development Plan.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statement under DBP Savings Account No. 0-010587-25-6 and book up the reconciling items. Henceforth, she should strictly observe Sections 74 and 97 of Presidential Decree 1445.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee conduct each offices to the physical inventory, determine the actual condition of these assets and render a report thereon pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Also, we recommend that all heads of departments prepare a list of all existing properties under their respective offices and submit the same to the Inventory Committee for validation and consolidation.
